meta.title: Asahidake Ski Course
meta.description: Ropeway × Daisetsuzan powder—northern Hokkaido's alpine ski mecca in a national park. Sugatami Pond, fumaroles, and Asahidake Onsen in one day.

hero.title:
Asahidake
Ski Course
hero.tagline: Ropeway × Daisetsuzan powder—northern Hokkaido's alpine ski sanctuary.

transit.title:
From Asahikawa
into the mountains.
transit.lead: About one hour by car from Asahikawa Airport, or roughly 85 minutes by bus from Asahikawa Station—a 101-passenger gondola climbs from 1,100 m at the base to 1,600 m at Sugatami in about 10 minutes. This is mountain skiing, not a groomed resort.
transit.imgAlt: Snow road toward the Daisetsuzan range—approach to Mt. Asahidake Ropeway

primary.title: Powder in a national park
primary.lead: Inside Daisetsuzan National Park, the ropeway runs from 1,100 m at the base to 1,600 m at Sugatami—ungroomed alpine terrain, not machine-packed runs. With Mt. Asahidake (2,291 m), Hokkaido's highest peak, as your backdrop, this is where dry Daisetsuzan powder earns its reputation.
primary.imgAlt: Powder slopes seen from Mt. Asahidake Ropeway—alpine skiing in the Daisetsuzan range

secondary.title: Fumaroles & Sugatami Pond
secondary.lead: At Sugatami, steam vents (fumaroles) dot the snowfield and the Sugatami Pond loop is a classic. Non-skiers can ride the ropeway for volcanic highland scenery and snow wellness walks.

recovery.title:
After skiing,
Asahidake Onsen.
recovery.lead: Sanzoku-yaki and Asahidake beer at Yukomanso, buffet dining at La Vista Daisetsuzan, local dishes at Kitoron—the hot-spring village at the ropeway base has classic après routes.
recovery.whisper: Soak at Yukomanso or La Vista Daisetsuzan's open-air bath with national-park snow views to warm up after a cold day.
recovery.imgAlt: Sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso—warming lunch after skiing

journey.title:
A full day from
Asahikawa
journey.lead: Alpine skiing, Sugatami walks, fumaroles, onsen, and photo-town Higashikawa—one loop through the Mt. Asahidake area.

01.title: Bus from Asahikawa
01.body: Asahidake Line bus to the ropeway base—about 85 minutes; early departures beat the crowds

02.title: Ropeway runs
02.body: Gondola to 1,600 m Sugatami and Daisetsuzan powder skiing

03.title: Onsen village lunch
03.body: Sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso, local set meals at Kitoron

04.title: Sugatami Pond loop
04.body: Winter walk past fumaroles and the alpine lake—ropeway-only visits possible (check official info)

05.title: Onsen & Higashikawa
05.body: Soak at Yukomanso and explore photo-town Higashikawa—a winter finale

wellness.title: Sugatami & fumarole walks
wellness.body: Ride the ropeway to the Sugatami deck—steam vents and Mt. Asahidake panoramas. Separate sightseeing routes may apply (confirm officially).
wellness.thumbAlt: Steam from fumaroles on the snowfield—volcanic scenery at Sugatami

walk.title: 85 minutes by bus from Asahikawa Station
walk.body: About one hour by car from Asahikawa Airport. Studless tires required in winter—watch for blizzards and low visibility inside the national park. Confirm operations and snow reports before you travel.

responsible.title: Alpine ski etiquette
responsible.body: Respect no-entry zones, sudden weather changes, and avalanche risk in the national park. This is not a groomed resort—check gear, fitness, and official closure notices before you go.

bluePond.title: Sugatami Pond — alpine mirror
bluePond.body: The Sugatami Pond loop reflects Mt. Asahidake and stays popular in winter. Steam from fumaroles over snowfields—volcanic scenery unique to Daisetsuzan.
bluePond.thumbAlt: Mt. Asahidake reflected in Sugatami Pond—winter alpine lake

food.title: Asahidake dining 10 — before & after
food.body: Sanzoku-yaki, local beer, mountain cuisine—hot-spring village picks for your ski day.
food.thumbAlt: Sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso—classic Asahidake Onsen lunch

onsen.title: Nearby onsen 10 — snow-view soaks
onsen.body: Yukomanso, La Vista Daisetsuzan, and Kitoron—day-trip baths after skiing.
onsen.thumbAlt: Snow and steam—warming up at Asahidake Onsen

featuredSpot.title: Sugatami Pond & fumaroles
featuredSpot.lead: Volcanic steam vents and an alpine lake reflecting Mt. Asahidake—after ropeway skiing.
featuredSpot.imgAlt: Steam from fumaroles and snowfields viewed from Sugatami

meta.title: Sugatami Pond & fumaroles — Asahidake Ski Course
meta.description: Alpine lake loop, volcanic scenery, ropeway walks—guide for international visitors

hero.title:
Sugatami Pond—
beyond the photo
hero.tagline: Daisetsuzan's volcanic scenery—after alpine skiing, into highland walks

bluePond.notice: Loop routes, ropeway operations, and snow conditions change daily. Confirm on the Mt. Asahidake Ropeway and national-park official sites before you go.
bluePond.introLead: Sugatami Pond mirrors Mt. Asahidake on still days. Active fumaroles nearby release steam over the snow—a landscape you cannot get at a groomed resort.
bluePond.introBody: The loop from 1,600 m Sugatami works with skiing or as a ropeway-only visit—signature volcanic scenery in Daisetsuzan National Park.
bluePond.whenLead: Typically mid-December through early May during ski season, clear mornings offer sharp outlines of Mt. Asahidake and reflections on the pond—the highland panorama international visitors seek most.
bluePond.whenNote: Blizzards, strong wind, or poor visibility may suspend the ropeway or close the loop.
bluePond.accessTitle: From the ropeway base & ski course
bluePond.accessLead: After skiing or by ropeway only to Sugatami. About 10 minutes from the base station—stay on designated loop routes around Sugatami.

drive.label: Car
drive.body: Asahikawa Airport → ropeway base, approx. 1 hour. Asahikawa Station → bus approx. 85 min. Winter tires required.

bus.label: Bus
bus.body: Asahidake Line from Asahikawa Station (limited daily departures). Winter schedules vary by year—check officially.

pair.label: Itinerary
pair.body: Ropeway skiing → Sugatami Pond loop → Yukomanso lunch → onsen soak in one day.

winter.label: Winter
winter.body: Watch for sudden weather inside the national park. Stay on marked routes near fumaroles and the pond.

bluePond.responsibleLead: Follow national-park rules—no dangerous behavior at fumaroles or the lakeshore. Stay on designated routes and yield to other walkers.
bluePond.mapsBody: Asahidake Onsen, Higashikawa-cho, Kamikawa-gun, Hokkaido. Check your route on Google Maps.
bluePond.heroImageAlt: Sugatami Pond in winter—alpine lake reflecting Mt. Asahidake
bluePond.galleryAlt: Fumaroles at Sugatami—steam and Daisetsuzan snowscape

meta.title: Snow Experiences — Asahidake Ski Course
meta.description: Sugatami Pond loop, ropeway walks, winter alpine tours—official links included

hero.title:
Mountain winter
without skiing
hero.tagline: Ropeway to Sugatami walks and fumaroles—touch Daisetsuzan winter

snowPlay.notice: Ropeway operations, loop routes, and prices change—the official site is authoritative. Entry restrictions may apply inside the national park due to weather or snow.
snowPlay.introLead: Asahidake Ski Course is an alpine-ski base. Even without skis, the ropeway and Sugatami walks let you experience Daisetsuzan winter.
snowPlay.introBody: Enjoy only in welcoming spaces—no entering restricted zones or approaching fumaroles off-route. Follow official guidance.
snowPlay.hubLead: A 101-passenger gondola climbs from 1,100 m to 1,600 m in about 10 minutes. Fumaroles and Mt. Asahidake panoramas—a highland entry without skis.
snowPlay.experiencesLead: For Sugatami loops and winter alpine programs, check the latest info on the official pages below.

furari.title: Sugatami Pond loop walk
furari.subtitle: Alpine lake hike from Sugatami station
furari.lead: Sugatami Pond reflecting Mt. Asahidake and steam from fumaroles—winter highland walking in deep snow. Confirm ropeway operations and routes officially.
furari.season: Typically Dec 11–May 10 (ski season; varies with snow)
furari.meet: Mt. Asahidake Ropeway base station · Bus from Asahikawa Station approx. 85 min
furari.duration: Ropeway round trip approx. 20 min plus loop (depends on fitness and weather)
furari.note: Inside a national park—operations may suspend in bad weather. Always check official ropeway status.

tokotoko.title: Higashikawa tourism info
tokotoko.subtitle: Photo town & Asahidake area itineraries
tokotoko.lead: From Asahidake Onsen to cafés, galleries, and lodging in town—check the official site for the latest winter programs.

snowPlay.pairingLead: AM: ropeway alpine skiing → Lunch: Sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso → PM: Sugatami Pond loop → Night: soak at Yukomanso.
snowPlay.heroImageAlt: Walking Sugatami snowfields—a Daisetsuzan snow wellness experience
snowPlay.galleryAlt: Fumarole steam and clear winter sky—Asahidake snow wellness scenery

meta.title: 10 Nearby Eats — Asahidake Ski Course
meta.description: Curated food picks and area map—pair with alpine skiing and Sugatami walks in one day

hero.title:
After the snow,
into mountain food
hero.tagline: Sanzoku-yaki, local beer, regional dishes—hot-spring village and Higashikawa eats for a seamless day

nearbyFood.notice: Hours, closures, and winter schedules change—each venue's official site is authoritative. No entering restricted national-park zones; enjoy only in welcoming spaces.
nearbyFood.introLead: Visitors to Asahidake—especially international visitors—are highly sensitive to Hokkaido mountain cuisine and volcanic alpine settings.
nearbyFood.introBody: We picked ten strong food stops before and after alpine skiing. Combined with ropeway walks and Sugatami Pond, they form a friction-free day-trip package.
nearbyFood.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and hub layers—show multiple at once.

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use View on map in the list below or Spot list on the map

nearbyFood.spotsTitle: 10 nearby eats that resonate with Asahidake visitors
nearbyFood.spotsLead: Grouped by category—easy to slot before or after skiing and walks.

junpei.title: Asahidake Onsen Yukomanso
junpei.lead: The icon of Asahidake Onsen. Signature sanzoku-yaki and house Asahidake beer draw long queues from core skiers to international visitors.

biei-farm.title: La Vista Daisetsuzan
biei-farm.lead: Resort hotel facing Mt. Asahidake. Buffet and course dining with alpine views—lodging and lunch in one scenic stop.

aruno.title: Asahidake Onsen Kitoron
aruno.lead: Classic wooden inn. Game and mountain-vegetable set meals—hits travelers who want authentic hot-spring village food culture.

chiyoda.title: Ropeway base restaurant
chiyoda.lead: Cafeteria at the gondola base. Curry and set meals—essential for skiers who want a quick meal before or after the lift.

ferme.title: Higashikawa cafés & galleries
ferme.lead: Cafés and galleries in photo-town Higashikawa. Daisetsuzan-themed exhibits and local lunches—a cultural stop on the way back.

between.title: Asahikawa Ramen Village
between.lead: Ramen cluster near Asahikawa Station. Shoyu, miso, shio—Japanese ramen is killer content for travelers. Warm up before or after the bus.

gosh.title: Higashikawa roast café
gosh.lead: Highly rated in photo-town Higashikawa—house-roasted coffee and handmade sweets to rest after cold mountain hours.

asperge.title: Asahidake highland restaurant
asperge.lead: Serious cuisine using Daisetsuzan ingredients—for higher-budget food travelers who want quality after skiing.

sabo.title: Asahikawa Station tea house
sabo.lead: Steps from Asahikawa Station. Hokkaido soft-serve and drinks—Japanese dessert cues that hook international visitors.

santouka.title: Ramen Santouka Asahikawa
santouka.lead: Famous birthplace of the chain. Salt ramen is known worldwide—essential warming food before or after a ski expedition.

nearbyFood.pairingLead: Asahikawa Station → bus to Asahidake → AM: alpine skiing → Lunch: sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso → PM: Sugatami Pond loop → Night: soak at Yukomanso. Rental car from Asahikawa Airport in about one hour.

meta.title: 10 Nearby Onsen — Asahidake Ski Course
meta.description: Snow-view open-air baths and day visits—onsen map paired with alpine skiing

hero.title:
Warm the body
in hot springs
hero.tagline: After alpine skiing and Sugatami walks—Japanese onsen culture and snow-view roten

nearbyOnsen.notice: Day-bath availability, hours, winter closures, and mixed-bath rules change—check each facility. Use extra care at wild roten baths.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: Visitors around Asahidake strongly want Japanese onsen culture and open-air baths in snowy scenery.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Ten baths that warm chilled bodies after alpine skiing and highland walks—designed as a digital bundle with our food map.
nearbyOnsen.mapLead: Toggle food, onsen, and hub layers—show multiple at once.

mapLayers.hint: Food and onsen can be shown together
mapLayers.mobileHint: If pins overlap, use View on map in the list below or Spot list on the map

nearbyOnsen.spotsTitle: 10 nearby onsen that resonate with Asahidake visitors
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Centered on Asahidake Onsen—easy to pair before/after alpine skiing and Sugatami walks.

ao-no-bi-yuyu.title: Asahidake Onsen Yukomanso
ao-no-bi-yuyu.lead: Famed bath at the foot of Mt. Asahidake—rustic wooden tubs and sanzoku-yaki. The standard stop for powder skiers and mountain visitors.

park-hills.title: La Vista Daisetsuzan
park-hills.lead: Open-air bath facing Mt. Asahidake. Large resort baths—a family and group favorite after skiing.

tsuewasure.title: Asahidake Onsen Kitoron
tsuewasure.lead: Calm free-flowing water in wooden baths and regional cuisine—for travelers seeking a real mountain onsen.

mori-no-ryotei.title: Asahidake Lodge
mori-no-ryotei.lead: Lodging near the ropeway base with day baths—handy for early-morning mountain access.

mori-no-shizuku.title: Sounkyo Onsen hotel
mori-no-shizuku.lead: Open baths over the Sounkyo gorge. An easy drive from Asahidake with reliable facilities.

kokumin.title: Asahidake day-bath facility
kokumin.lead: Day-use baths in the hot-spring village—an unadorned mountain soak after skiing.

ryounkaku.title: Tenninkyo Onsen (Higashikawa)
ryounkaku.lead: Secluded gorge onsen reachable by car from Asahidake—deep snow and steam for strong photo contrast.

hakuginso.title: Sounkyo large hot-spring hotel
hakuginso.lead: Major Sounkyo facility with wide baths and sauna—a stop on the way back toward Asahikawa.

fukiage-roten.title: Higashikawa outdoor bath
fukiage-roten.lead: Open-air bath in the Daisetsuzan foothills—adventure soaking with extra care for manners and safety.

yukoma.title: Asahikawa city onsen & sento
yukoma.lead: Day baths near Asahikawa Station—warm up in the city before or after the bus leg of a ski trip.

nearbyOnsen.pairingLead: AM: ropeway alpine skiing → Lunch: sanzoku-yaki at Yukomanso → PM: Sugatami Pond loop → Evening: soak at Yukomanso → Night: stay in Asahikawa (extension).

access.line: Yukomanso 1418, Asahidake Onsen, Higashikawa-cho, Kamikawa-gun, Hokkaido
access.note: ~85 min by bus from Asahikawa Station · ~1 hr by car from Asahikawa Airport · Ropeway 1,100 m→1,600 m · Gondola for ski ticket holders only
